description: Database migration creates the brews table with correct schema
steps: Run the SQL in scripts/migrate.sql against the Vercel Postgres instance
Verify the brews table exists
Verify columns: id (uuid), bean_name (text), grams (decimal), brew_time (integer), grind_setting (integer), comments (text nullable), created_at (timestamptz)
Verify id defaults to gen_random_uuid()
Verify created_at defaults to NOW()

description: Page renders with correct botanical parchment design
steps: Navigate to the app
Verify the background is warm parchment (#F2EBD3)
Verify a subtle grain texture overlay is visible
Verify the heading 'Brew' is displayed in Playfair Display font
Verify body text uses Inter font
Verify a horizontal divider separates the header from the table

description: Header contains title and New Entry button
steps: Navigate to the app
Verify 'Brew' title appears on the left of the header
Verify 'New Entry' button appears on the right of the header
Verify the button uses the accent green color (#3D5C37)

description: Empty state is shown when no brews exist
steps: Ensure the database has no rows
Navigate to the app
Verify no table rows are rendered
Verify an empty state message is visible

description: Brews table displays all entries sorted newest first
steps: Add at least two brew entries at different times
Navigate to the app
Verify the most recently created brew appears at the top of the table
Verify all entries are present

description: Table displays all required columns
steps: Add a brew entry with all fields filled
Navigate to the app
Verify the table has columns: Bean Name, Dose (g), Time, Grind, Comments, Date, and an actions column
Verify each column shows the correct data for the entry

description: Brew time is displayed as mm:ss in the table
steps: Add a brew with a brew time of 3 minutes 45 seconds
Navigate to the app
Verify the time column displays '3:45' for that entry

description: Date is displayed as a readable formatted date in the table
steps: Add a brew entry
Navigate to the app
Verify the Date column shows a human-readable date and time (e.g. 'Apr 15, 2026, 08:30 PM')
Verify it does not show a raw ISO timestamp

description: New Entry button opens the create dialog
steps: Navigate to the app
Click the 'New Entry' button
Verify a modal dialog appears
Verify the dialog title indicates creating a new brew
Verify all form fields are empty

description: Create dialog contains all required fields
steps: Open the create dialog
Verify a 'Bean Name' text input is present
Verify a 'Dose (g)' number input is present
Verify a 'Brew Time' input with mm:ss format is present
Verify a 'Grind Setting' number input (0–50) is present
Verify a 'Comments' textarea is present
Verify a submit button is present

description: Submitting the create form adds the brew to the table
steps: Open the create dialog
Fill in Bean Name: 'Ethiopia Yirgacheffe'
Fill in Dose: '15.5'
Fill in Brew Time: '3:45'
Fill in Grind Setting: '24'
Fill in Comments: 'Tastes bright and citrusy'
Click the submit button
Verify the dialog closes
Verify the new entry appears at the top of the table with correct values

description: created_at timestamp is set automatically on insert
steps: Create a new brew entry
Verify the Date column is populated automatically
Verify the timestamp was not a user-editable field in the form

description: Edit icon per row opens the edit dialog pre-populated
steps: Ensure at least one brew entry exists in the table
Click the pencil (edit) icon for a row
Verify the dialog opens
Verify the dialog title indicates editing
Verify each field is pre-populated with the row's current values
Verify brew time is shown as mm:ss in the input

description: Submitting the edit form updates the brew in the table
steps: Open the edit dialog for an existing brew
Change the Bean Name to a new value
Submit the form
Verify the dialog closes
Verify the table row reflects the updated Bean Name
Verify other fields remain unchanged

description: Delete icon per row shows a confirmation dialog
steps: Ensure at least one brew entry exists in the table
Click the trash (delete) icon for a row
Verify a confirmation dialog appears
Verify the dialog asks for confirmation before deleting

description: Confirming delete removes the brew from the table
steps: Click the trash icon for a row
Click the confirm/delete button in the confirmation dialog
Verify the confirmation dialog closes
Verify the deleted row no longer appears in the table

description: Cancelling the delete confirmation leaves the brew unchanged
steps: Click the trash icon for a row
Click the cancel button in the confirmation dialog
Verify the confirmation dialog closes
Verify the row is still present in the table

description: Dialog closes when pressing the ESC key
steps: Open the create dialog
Press the ESC key
Verify the dialog closes

description: Dialog closes when clicking the backdrop
steps: Open the create dialog
Click outside the dialog on the backdrop
Verify the dialog closes

description: Dialog focuses the first input field on open
steps: Open the create dialog
Verify focus is automatically placed on the Bean Name input
